CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-16-2014 Continued To Dec-17-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Defendants Notice Of Motion And Motion For Relief From Entry Of Default
Off Calendar Oct-02-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT HUGO GAMBOA's Motion For Relief From Entry Of Default is Granted. (CCP sec. 473(b).) "[I]t has been held that where the aggrieved party makes a strong showing of diligence in seeking relief after discovery of facts, and the other party is unable to show prejudice from the delay, the original negligence in allowing the default to be taken will be excused on a weak showing." (Aldrich v. San Fernando Valley Lumber Co. (1985) 170 Cal.App.3d 725, 740.) Defendant has demonstrated diligence, as he has moved to set aside the default within two months of its entry. Plaintiff has not established that he will be prejudiced if the default is set aside. Defendant has established excusable mistake and/or neglect by relying on Plaintiff's successor-in-interest's representation that the lawsuit would not be pursued. Defendant shall pay Plaintiff's costs for filing and service in the amount of $937.54 within 30 days of entry of this Order. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/ehg
